ELPAC Level 1
Lexile 1300–1400L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Captions for the Cedar Basket

STS-MS7-L1-13001400-MSELA7-MEC-IFN-02 • MS ELA Grade 7 • Text Features in Public Documents
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Museum curators decide how visitors will find information before the doors ever open. Headings tell you what an exhibit is about, captions explain each object, and a map legend unlocks every symbol on the page. Many First Nations communities have shared knowledge for generations through objects like cedar baskets, canoes, and drums, and a good exhibit lets those objects teach.
If you could add one more text feature to help a visitor understand the cedar basket, what would it be and why?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Kaya doing?
Look at the picture. Say what Kaya is doing. Use the exact action word, not "doing something."
2
What is Tomah holding?
Look at the picture. Say what Tomah is holding. Use the exact name, not "a thing."
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say what the teacher is doing. Use the exact action word.
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say what kind of room this is. Use the exact room name.
5
Tell me about the exhibit board.
Look at the picture. Say one thing the exhibit board shows. Use the exact name, not "stuff."
6
What is Aiyana wearing?
Look at the picture. Say what Aiyana is wearing. Use the exact color and clothing word.
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS RI.7.5 — Analyze Text Structure and Features in Public Documents. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
